tool_name: Chiller Plant Free-Cooling Economiser — Switchover Temperature, Annual Hours & Energy Saving

inputs.name: Chiller Full-Load COP (rated condition)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7.3 — Default Type B: ±5% for unclassified parameters

inputs.name: Cooling Tower / Dry Cooler Approach Temperature (ΔT_approach)
inputs.note: Wet cooling tower: approach = T_CW,out − T_wb; dry cooler: approach = T_fluid,out − T_db

inputs.name: Plate Heat Exchanger Temperature Approach (ΔT_HX)
inputs.note: Brazed plate HX: ΔT_HX = 1.5–3 K; gasketed frame: 2–5 K

inputs.name: Design Peak Cooling Load (Q_design)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 286-1:2010 §4 — Design specification nominal, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Annual Full-Load Equivalent Cooling Hours (FLEH)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Discrete count variable, zero measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Site Climate Zone — Bin Hours Below Switchover
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

inputs.name: Electricity Tariff
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 21748:2017 §7 — Market parameter Type B: cost/price estimates ±5%

formulas: T_wb_sw_c = chilled_water_supply_c - approach_temp_k - heat_exchanger_dT_k  // [°C] — Switchover Wet-Bulb Temperature (T_wb,sw) — Ref: T_wb,sw = T_CHWS − ΔT_approach − ΔT_HX — ASHRAE 90.1-2022 §6.5.1.1; below this outdoor WB, free-cooling is feasible
h_FC = Math.min(site_climate_zone, annual_cooling_hours)  // [h/yr] — Annual Free-Cooling Hours (h_FC) — Ref: Free-cooling hours = min(bin hours below T_wb,sw, total cooling hours) — CIBSE TM21 §4.2
F_FC = h_FC / annual_cooling_hours  // Free-Cooling Fraction (F_FC) — Ref: Fraction of annual cooling delivered without mechanical refrigeration

E_chiller_base_kwh = design_cooling_load_kw / chiller_COP * annual_cooling_hours  // [kWh/yr] — Annual Chiller Energy — Baseline (E_chiller_base) — Ref: Baseline: all cooling via chiller at full-load COP — ASHRAE 90.1 §11 baseline building

pump_fan_parasitic_kw = design_cooling_load_kw * 0.03; E_saving_kwh = (design_cooling_load_kw / chiller_COP - pump_fan_parasitic_kw) * h_FC  // [kWh/yr] — Annual Energy Saving with Free-Cooling (E_saving) — Ref: E_saving = (Q/COP − P_parasitic) × h_FC; parasitic = 3% of load for CT fan + HX pump — CIBSE TM21 §5

cost_saving_usd = E_saving_kwh * electricity_rate_usd_kwh; HX_cost_est_usd = design_cooling_load_kw * 40; payback_yr = HX_cost_est_usd / cost_saving_usd  // [USD/yr, years] — Annual Cost Saving & Simple Payback vs HX Capital — Ref: HX installed cost ≈ 40 USD/kW (brazed plate, 2024 estimate); payback = CAPEX / annual saving

rules.message: Switchover WB temperature below 0°C in warm climate: free-cooling hours will be negligible — evaluate dry cooler or adiabatic pre-cooling to raise T_wb,sw
Very low CHWS temp with tight HX approach: fouling will increase ΔT_HX rapidly — specify minimum HX surface oversize of 20% per TEMA C
Free-cooling fraction > 80%: chiller operates less than 20% of time — evaluate chiller downsizing or VFD for part-load optimisation; chiller may fall below minimum unload point

smart_warnings.message: Free-cooling payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritise HX installation in next capital cycle; consider controls upgrade for automatic switchover per ASHRAE 90.1 §6.5.1
Warm climate with few free-cooling hours: marginal economic case — evaluate night-time free-cooling for data centres where T_wb is lower overnight
High cooling tower approach (>6 K): tower fouled or undersized — cleaning or replacement can recover 2–3 K and extend free-cooling season significantly
Free-cooling saves >50% of baseline chiller energy: very favourable climate — evaluate tri-mode (full/partial/zero mechanical) controls for transitional hours
Low-efficiency chiller in cold climate: both free-cooling AND chiller replacement are warranted — combined ROI should be modelled jointly

fmea.effect: Switchover WB temperature below 0°C in warm climate: free-cooling hours will be negligible — evaluate dry cooler or adiabatic pre-cooling to raise T_wb,sw
Very low CHWS temp with tight HX approach: fouling will increase ΔT_HX rapidly — specify minimum HX surface oversize of 20% per TEMA C
Free-cooling fraction > 80%: chiller operates less than 20% of time — evaluate chiller downsizing or VFD for part-load optimisation; chiller may fall below minimum unload point

fmea.failureMode: Free-cooling payback < 2 years: strong ROI — prioritise HX installation in next capital cycle; consider controls upgrade for automatic switchover per ASHRAE 90.1 §6.5.1
Warm climate with few free-cooling hours: marginal economic case — evaluate night-time free-cooling for data centres where T_wb is lower overnight
High cooling tower approach (>6 K): tower fouled or undersized — cleaning or replacement can recover 2–3 K and extend free-cooling season significantly
Free-cooling saves >50% of baseline chiller energy: very favourable climate — evaluate tri-mode (full/partial/zero mechanical) controls for transitional hours
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: E_saving_kwh > E_chiller_base_kwh * 0.5

fmea.failureMode: Low-efficiency chiller in cold climate: both free-cooling AND chiller replacement are warranted — combined ROI should be modelled jointly
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: chiller_COP < 4 && site_climate_zone > 2000
